records.ety: [OE. destroien, destruien, destrien, OF. destruire, F. dtruire, fr. L. destruere, destructum; de + struere to pile up, build. See Structure.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Destroyed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Destroying.]

defs: To unbuild; to pull or tear down; to separate virulently into its constituent parts; to break up the structure and organic existence of; to demolish.
quotes: But ye shall destroy their altars, break their images, and cut down their groves.

defs: To ruin; to bring to naught; to put an end to; to annihilate; to consume.
quotes: I will utterly pluck up and destroy that nation.

defs: To put an end to the existence, prosperity, or beauty of; to kill.
quotes: If him by force he can destroy, or, worse, By some false guile pervert.
